title: Anisophyllum dioecum
uses: Anisophyllum dioecum, also known as the prickly bush, has been traditionally used in various folk medicines for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's leaves and stems are often used to treat wounds, cuts, and skin infections. In some cultures, Anisophyllum dioecum is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential antifungal and antioxidant properties. However, further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential health benefits.

Anisophyllea dioica is a dioecious plant species within the family Anisophylleaceae. The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help protect against cell damage and alleviate symptoms associated with certain diseases. The bioactive constituents of Anisophyllea dioica include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, among others. Anisophyllea dioica is used to prepare various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
